condition.selected: The unknown is always the hardest number

params.description: All three numbers highlight across the equations

steps.text: The unknown changes position, but that does not change the numbers themselves. Look at what the numbers mean together. What relationship do 4, 5, and 20 show?

condition.selected: The numbers are different each time

incorrects.description: Student selected option C: The numbers are different each time

params.description: The numbers 4, 5, and 20 highlight across all three equations

steps.text: Look closely at the numbers. Each equation uses 4, 5, and 20. The unknown moves, but the numbers stay the same. What do those three numbers mean together?

condition.selected: The equation changes meaning each time

incorrects.description: Student selected option D: The equation changes meaning each time

params.description: The numbers 4, 5, and 20 highlight across all three equations

steps.text: The position of the unknown changes, but the meaning does not. All three equations show the same relationship. Read what 4, 5, and 20 mean together.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: The numbers 4, 5, and 20 pulse in sequence across all three equations, showing the consistent relationship

steps.text: Let me show you what stays the same. In every equation, 4 groups of 5 equals 20. That relationship does not change. The unknown moves to different positions, but the fact that 4 groups of 5 makes 20 is always true. The answer is: 4 groups of 5 equals 20.
